perturbation.original_text: This Promotion Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of February 3, 2010 ("Effective Date") by and between MiddleBrook Pharmaceuticals, Inc. ("MBRK") , a Delaware corporation with offices at 7 Village Circle, Suite 100, Westlake, TX 76262 and DoctorDirectory.com, Inc. ("DD") , a South Carolina Corporation, with offices at One Page Avenue, Suite 280, Asheville, NC 28801.
perturbation.changed_text: This Distribution Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of February 3, 2010 ("Effective Date") by and between MiddleBrook Pharmaceuticals, Inc. ("MBRK") , a Delaware corporation with offices at 7 Village Circle, Suite 100, Westlake, TX 76262 and DoctorDirectory.com, Inc. ("DD") , a South Carolina Corporation, with offices at One Page Avenue, Suite 280, Asheville, NC 28801.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Promotion Agreement" to "Distribution Agreement" in the introductory paragraph introduces confusion. Later sections refer to 'Promotion Fees' and promotional activities which are now inconsistent with the agreement being defined as a Distribution Agreement. This leads to uncertainty about the actual nature of the agreement and the expected services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3. Additionally, on or before the last day of each month, DD will invoice MBRK for the Promotion Fees due for the prior month. Such Promotion Fees shall be calculated by: (a) the following formula for the period from the Effective Date through September 30, 2010: For example: if during the month of March 2010 (a) (A) above was 2,000 TRx and (B) above was $36.50 then MBRK would be remit $36,500.00 to DD. OR (b) the following formula for the period from October 1, 2010 through termination of this Agreement: (A) the Actual DD Target Segment MOXATAG TRx for the Promotional Measurement Period multiplied by: (B) the applicable Gross Margin Per TRx multiplied by: (C) 50%. (A) the Actual DD Target Segment MOXATAG TRx Tablets for the Promotional Measurement Period multiplied by: (B) the applicable Gross Margin Per Tablet multiplied by: (C) 50%. For example: if during the month of November 2010: MBRK would remit $89,425.00 to DD: These Promotion Fees represent fair market value payment for such services rendered. The Parties recognize that Promotion Fees are to be settled on a monthly basis which requires the use of an estimated Gross Margin Per Tablet amount until the actual Gross Margin Per Tablet for an applicable quarter can be determined. Accordingly, to facilitate the calculation and settlement of monthly Promotion Fees for periods after September 2010, the Parties agree that the Gross Margin Per Tablet used to settle the monthly Promotion Fees will be that as calculated using the previous calendar quarter's Gross Margin Per Tablet data and current period's Actual DD Target Segment MOXATAG TRx. Such Promotion Fees for the three months in any calendar quarter will be subject to a true-up process which will occur by the 15th of the second month of the following quarter. By way of example, Promotion Fees for each of the months of April, May and June will be settled using the Gross Margin Per Tablet amount that has been calculated for the quarter ended March 31 and will be subject to a true-up process to occur by August 15 with any adjustment to such Promotion Fees being settled between the parties within 15 days thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Promotion Agreement" to "Distribution Agreement" creates a contradiction with section 5.3 which refers to "Promotion Fees" and calculations related to promotional activities. If the agreement is now defined as a Distribution Agreement, the references to 'Promotion Fees' become inconsistent and unclear, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the interchangeable use of terms without clear definitions leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: DD will promote MOXATAG to DD Target Segment Prescribers using its full suite of promotion solutions where DD and MBRK deem appropriate, including but not limited to DD's a) eSampling Platform, b) eLearning / eDetailing, c) Educational Email, d) Direct-to-Physician Bulletin Services, e) Physician Portal Promotions, f) Patient Portal Promotions, g) Direct Mail Advertising, h) Coupon Promotions, i) Consumer Condition Content, j) Search Engine Marketing and k) Mini Web Site Promotions where appropriate and with prior written approval from MBRK.
perturbation.changed_text: DD will market MOXATAG to DD Target Segment Prescribers using its full suite of marketing solutions where DD and MBRK deem appropriate, including but not limited to DD's a) eSampling Platform, b) eLearning / eDetailing, c) Educational Email, d) Direct-to-Physician Bulletin Services, e) Physician Portal Promotions, f) Patient Portal Promotions, g) Direct Mail Advertising, h) Coupon Promotions, i) Consumer Condition Content, j) Search Engine Marketing and k) Mini Web Site Promotions where appropriate and with prior written approval from MBRK.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "promote" to "market" introduces a subtle but significant contradiction. 'Promotion' is specifically defined later. By using "market" instead, the agreement becomes vague as to whether DD is expected to perform promotion or general marketing services, which can have different implications for compliance and scope of work.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (v) "Promotional Materials" shall mean all MBRK-approved and available sales representative training materials and all MBRK-approved and available written, printed, graphic, electronic, audio or video matter, including, but not limited to, journal advertisements, sales aids, formulary binders, reprints, direct mail, direct-to-consumer advertising, Internet postings, broadcast advertisements and sales reminder aids (for example, scratch pads, pens and other such items), in each case created by a party or on its behalf and used or intended for use by DD and MBRK in connection with any promotion of MOXATAG hereunder, or disease state or indication for which MOXATAG is approved for treatment but excluding MOXATAG Labeling.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'promote,' which is later explicitly defined as 'Promotional Materials.' The changed text uses 'market' instead of 'promote.' This introduces ambiguity because 'market' is a broader term and is not defined in the contract. The contradicted text defines promotional materials and implies that only promotional activities are to be undertaken. The change creates a contradiction because marketing activities may or may not include promotion, making it unclear if DD is obligated to only use promotional materials or has a wider scope of marketing activities, thus directly conflicting with the scope established by the definition of 'Promotional Materials'.

perturbation.original_text: All materials (including Promotional Materials), documents, information, descriptions and suggestions of every kind supplied to DD by MBRK or any other affiliate of MBRK in connection with and/or pursuant to this Agreement or relationship established between DD and MBRK (including, without limitation, any such materials (including Promotional Materials), documents, information, descriptions and suggestions supplied to DD by MBRK prior to the execution of this Agreement) shall be the sole and exclusive property of MBRK and MBRK shall have the right to make whatever use it deems desirable of any such materials, documents, information, descriptions and suggestions.
perturbation.changed_text: All materials (including Marketing Materials), documents, information, descriptions and suggestions of every kind supplied to DD by MBRK or any other affiliate of MBRK in connection with and/or pursuant to this Agreement or relationship established between DD and MBRK (including, without limitation, any such materials (including Marketing Materials), documents, information, descriptions and suggestions supplied to DD by MBRK prior to the execution of this Agreement) shall be the sole and exclusive property of MBRK and MBRK shall have the right to make whatever use it deems desirable of any such materials, documents, information, descriptions and suggestions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Promotional Materials' to 'Marketing Materials' in Section 11.1 creates inconsistency with Section 9.2, which specifically mentions 'Promotional Materials.' It is now ambiguous whether the indemnification covers all materials or only those specifically defined as 'Promotional Materials' as the start of the agreement defines it as promotional materials and Section 11.1 is reffering to marketing materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.2. DD warrants that any Promotional Materials developed by DD or its Representatives under this Agreement, do not infringe, misappropriate or otherwise violate any Intellectual Property Rights of any third party, and agrees to protect, indemnify and hold harmless MBRK and its Representatives against any and all claims, losses, demands, causes of action, and any and all related costs and expenses including, but not limited to reasonable attorneys' fees, costs, and expenses, incurred by them in connection with any assertion for such infringement, misappropriation or breach.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Promotional Materials' to 'Marketing Materials' in the changed text introduces ambiguity as the original agreement defines materials in section 9.2 as 'Promotional Materials'. This creates a contradiction because it's now unclear whether Section 9.2, which refers to 'Promotional Materials' covers 'Marketing Materials' or not. This inconsistency creates uncertainty about DD's indemnification obligations and whether they extend to a broader set of materials now classified as 'Marketing Materials' potentially increasing liability for DD, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.
